title: Censor Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Censor Evangelion             Quote Post by Knoxer » Wed Jul 11, 2007 1:38 pm Okay, well I thought this would be a fun little thing to do and to make you think of how hard making something like NGE suitable for 4kids tv. Heres how it's going to happen.. You have just landed a job at 4KidsTv as a top editor, it's your job to find good animes that are getting a lot of response in Japan and some state side and censor them to make them suitable for kids. Now have chosen Evangelion knowing full well this task will be a hard one, but if done right will get to a big promotion. Now what you need to do is censor Evangelion and the movie to make it suitable for kids ages 6+. List what you would edit, scrap, and keep. Have fun!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 11, 2007 1:45 pm It's not even remotely possible, as the daytime airing on CN shows. Evangelion would suffer the same fate as Escaflowne. They would get half-way through the series before editing it into a hole. They would make it to about episode 21 before they would can it. See also: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/edit-list/2003-02-24/evangelion-ep-1 http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/edit-list/2003-02-26/evangelion-ep-2

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 11, 2007 1:51 pm I forgot who(m), but haven't there been other times that when Eva was shown it has been censored here in the U.S.?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Jul 11, 2007 3:08 pm Cartoon Network showed the first two episodes several years back during something they called "Giant Robot's Week" and they edited it pretty badly. Made me never want to watch that channel again. Well that and the crappy other anime they showed.

[]:             Quote Post by JP » Wed Jul 11, 2007 6:09 pm Man...it would literally be impossible. I remember for The Big O, Toonami changed a major phrase from the show, "Cast in the name of God - Ye not guilty" to "Cast in the name of Good..." You'd probably have to take out the crosses, Lilith, blood spraying. It would be a nightmare.

[]:             Quote Post by Knoxer » Wed Jul 11, 2007 6:51 pm Come on it's no fun if no one tries, and me and my friends have also said they would never be able to edit the whole series, if they did they would have a heart attack with EoE. But still trying is kinda fun in a way Edit: Saw the links made... dear god... it's not even eva anymore...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 11, 2007 8:22 pm According to the above SAUCE, the cross-shaped explosions were left in.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Jul 11, 2007 8:30 pm They would definitely have to censor out Kaworu's raging gayness.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 11, 2007 8:39 pm ....Or lack thereof? :mrgreen: (Bwahahahaha!)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jul 11, 2007 8:42 pm Bah! Kaworu wasn't even there in those episodes, anyway! This is sick...they cut poor pen-pen out. Too many cuts, it's useless. "Giant Robot Week." They should've had the Jet Alone episode, that was a robot proper and not a giant human that looks like a robot.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Jul 11, 2007 9:10 pm Hunter21 wrote: Cartoon Network showed the first two episodes several years back during something they called "Giant Robot's Week" and they edited it pretty badly. Made me never want to watch that channel again. Well that and the crappy other anime they showed.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 11, 2007 9:12 pm Hell, if only they would air EoE! But if that would happen, it'd most likely be like this, being an R-rated film (I *think* in Japan, both the series & Death/EoE probably had the same type of "rating" as opposed to the series being 15+ and EoE being 17+ in the States) and all: "Help me. Help me, Asuka." *cut cut cut* "...I feel sick." The End.

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Wed Jul 11, 2007 9:17 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It's not even remotely possible, as the daytime airing on CN shows. Evangelion would suffer the same fate as Escaflowne. They would get half-way through the series before editing it into a hole. They would make it to about episode 21 before they would can it. See also: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/edit-list/2003-02-24/evangelion-ep-1 http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/edit-list/2003-02-26/evangelion-ep-2

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Jul 11, 2007 9:18 pm "Help me. Help me, Asuka." *cut cut cut* "...I feel sick." The End. [/quote] With clever editing on that part it would actually work with out seeing Shinji's Baby Batter and Asuka's *slightly less inapproite breasts. No The reason why it won't air is due to all the scenes following the Giant Marshmellow flavored Rei. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 11, 2007 10:04 pm Well, the rights to telecast EoE in the US is currently in the hands of those at Starz Entertainment Group, so if it were to be shown on TV, it would be either on Starz or, more likely, Encore Action. That is; unless, there is a change in ownership rights. But I would really like to see Eva on Adult Swim again. Hell, I would like to see it on that new anime block on Sci-Fi, shining on the dumb-arses at CN!

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Jul 11, 2007 10:28 pm Censoring EVA reminds me of Cromartie High School for some odd reason... If you??ve seen it, maybe you??ll know what I mean... horrid.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Wed Jul 11, 2007 11:06 pm Opening scene...Sachiel swimming up, bird flies off....then Asuka on the beach saying "I feel sick".

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Jul 11, 2007 11:32 pm Shinji = Shane Rei = Rachel Asuka = Ashley

[]:             Quote Post by Snowshadow Falcon » Wed Jul 11, 2007 11:44 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Shinji = Shane

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jul 12, 2007 9:00 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: Well, the rights to telecast EoE in the US is currently in the hands of those at Starz Entertainment Group, so if it were to be shown on TV, it would be either on Starz or, more likely, Encore Action.
